author: JB
text: Want me to **push `main` to origin** and **delete the merged branch**? And whenever you're ready for the next hunt, I'd re-baseline first, then aim it at the now-fattest phases (steering ~13.5 ms, movement ~8.8 ms in your snapshot).

author: JB
text: push, delete branch etc...

author: JB
text: Nice result — that's the gate-sharpening work paying off end to end: from "every hunt finds nothing" to a soak-validated win in the engine.

author: JB
text: yes do we need to update perf-hunt with new expectations/baseline? it should really do that dynamically before a run right?

author: JB
text: Is there a way that grid_rebuild could just be completely removed from the tick budget and run completely independently?

author: JB
text: yes do we need to update perf-hunt with new expectations/baseline? it should really do that dynamically before a run right?

author: JB
text: The one thing I deliberately left alone: `path-to-one-million.md`'s phase numbers are still the old figures, but since the workflow now overrides them with live data, they're just historical reference. I can refresh them if you want the doc itself accurate for human reading, but it no longer affects hunt targeting.

author: JB
text: Is there a way that we could completely remove grid building from the tick budget by having them run completley independently, like their own seperate rust/bevy system and they just share a buffer, like we do with front/backend, with the rest of the system, so grid building never gets in the way of a tick / sim loop?

author: JB
text: My recommendation is **#1** — it targets the actual cost and the groundwork exists.

PERF HUNT — 2026-06-25 — EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

Baseline: wall p99 44.663 ms of 50 ms budget (noise σ 0.257 ms). Fattest phase: steering 13.6 ms (~30%). 5 ideas implemented, 0 KEEPs. Merge nothing.

MERGE SHORTLIST: Nothing clears the bank bar — recommend merging nothing this run. No candidate produced a wall-p99 win outside its noise floor at 1M.
- Closest: movement-atan2-free-turn-limit (only one with phase AND wall both negative; ships unit tests) — merge only inside a hand-merged movement-loop rewrite, after a determinism canary.
- movement-kill-powf, turn-rate-divide half only — bit-identical/zero-risk, fold opportunistically into that same rewrite; skip the max_speed half unless the canary clears it.

KEY FINDINGS: (1) The shared-collect ditch isolated the steering fork-join BARRIER (not the redundant 1M collect) as the real wall cost — load-bearing for the next steering attempt. (2) The two movement DEFERs are mutually exclusive as authored (both rewrite the same turn-limiter block + import line); the bundle could not stack. (3) The size-dominance Golden-Zone idea washed at the noise floor, reconfirming ledger #15.

HUNT NEXT: attack the steering fork-join barrier itself; hand-merge the two movement DEFERs into one loop and re-A/B; re-author the Bevy par_iter idea against the correct bevy_ecs 0.14.2 import path (BatchingStrategy is not at that path)."}</result>
